content: Renal dose adjustment strategies: reduce individual dose while maintaining frequency (gentamicin); extend dosing interval while maintaining dose (vancomycin); or both. CrCl-based dosing guidelines available in prescribing information. For CrCl 30-50: typically 50-75% of normal dose; CrCl 10-30: 25-50% of normal dose; CrCl <10: avoid or use minimal doses with monitoring. Dialysis considerations: schedule post-dialysis dosing for dialyzable drugs (aminoglycosides, vancomycin); supplemental doses needed after dialysis. Avoid nephrotoxic combinations (NSAIDs + ACE inhibitors + diuretics = triple whammy). Metformin: reduce dose if eGFR 30-45; contraindicated <30. DOACs: apixaban safest in CKD; avoid dabigatran if CrCl <30.
